name: The Standing Moot
nativeLogic: The heart-grove where the eldest Ents convene. Slow-time runs DEEP: a single Moot can take a human lifetime to conclude, a single speech a full day. To petition the Moot is to commit to patience beyond human endurance — unless you're an Ent, to whom this is simply how decisions are made. The Rootbound-vs-Walker argument is decided here, glacially. GM enforces slow-time as law: nothing is rushed, bribed, or deceived at speed; haste is answered by the grove itself.
descriptionSeed: The oldest grove — Ents vast as hills standing in a ring that has held its shape for centuries, their voices a slow grinding music that takes a day to finish a thought. Here the Deepwood decides what it will be: sealed against humankind, or woven into the world. The very air is patient. A human could grow old waiting for a verdict; an Ent settles in and adds their voice to a debate their grandparents began.
encounterFlavor: Glacial deliberation — an argument centuries old, a verdict that will outlive everyone waiting for it, the profound patience of beings who think in ages, the rare weight of being allowed to add your voice.
questSeeds: Petition the Moot to keep the Green Accord — the Green Schism arc's heart; only an Ent, or a human of monstrous patience, can bear the pace to argue it, and winning turns the Rootbound tide
The Rootbound are close to a sealing verdict; finding what would move even one elder Walker-ward is the long game
An Ent-character can claim standing at the Moot by lineage or deed — earning the right to speak is its own arc
axisVectorNote: Derived from authored poleIntensity against world_node_atlas.axisOrder (SNG-180). Disposition — what this place IS. Distinct from world position.
